channel.title: Posts on Akila Welihinda
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on Akila Welihinda

item.title: Simplifying the GNU C Sine Function
item.description: In this post I will discuss the story behind my first open-source contribution . Have you ever wondered how computers calculate trigonometric functions like sin and cos?

item.title: How Scala Changed My Perspective on Programming
item.description: My last job required me to learn Scala, which is considered to be a functional-OOP hybrid language. Seeing real-world applications of Scala was what ultimately sold me on the language’s concepts, and now I’m very grateful for the big perspective shift Scala has given me.

item.title: Tools That Improved My Engineering Productivity
item.description: My friends and coworkers often ask me what tools I use because they know I am a productivity enthusiast. I have always enjoyed improving my productivity because the feeling of efficiency makes working more enjoyable for me.

item.title: Exploring SIMD performance improvements in WebAssembly
item.description: In this blog post, we are going to run some SIMD (Single Instruction Multiple Data) performance experiments in WebAssembly and see whether the results agree with our theoretical predictions.

item.title: Click: My Primary Kubernetes Tool
item.description: Click has made my Kubernetes experience a pleasure. I adopted Click after joining Databricks because using kubectl was too inconvenient.

item.title: My Journey at Databricks
item.description: Finding Databricks When I was working on the batch scheduling team at Facebook, I noticed something unusual about our sister team.
